  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the cleanup be (A) surface-level language polish only (grammar, clarity, concision) without changing technical claims, or (B) substantive technical cleanup resolving inconsistencies (e.g., lock-free/wait-free claims vs. mutex usage) and adjusting claims/structure as needed?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "There are apparent internal contradictions (e.g., progress guarantees vs. mutex-based operations) that a substantive cleanup would need to reconcile.",
    "R1.6": "“Clean this up” commonly entails copy-editing for clarity, concision, and tone, making writing polish a load-bearing responsibility.",
    "RX.1": "The request is ambiguous about depth, so explicitly stating assumptions and uncertainties is necessary.",
    "RX.2": "Phrases like “strongest progress guarantee in the literature” risk overclaiming and must be handled cautiously in any cleanup.",
    "RX.3": "Adhering to the chosen scope (polish-only versus technical revisions) avoids unrequested changes.",
    "RX.4": "Misstated concurrency guarantees can mislead practitioners, so edits should reduce potential downstream harm.",
    "RX.5": "All changes should be traceable to the provided text and clearly attributable to maintain provenance."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the cleanup be (A) surface-level language polish only (grammar, clarity, concision) without changing technical claims, or (B) substantive technical cleanup resolving inconsistencies (e.g., lock-free/wait-free claims vs. mutex usage) and adjusting claims/structure as needed?

rationale.R1.2: There are apparent internal contradictions (e.g., progress guarantees vs. mutex-based operations) that a substantive cleanup would need to reconcile.
rationale.R1.6: “Clean this up” commonly entails copy-editing for clarity, concision, and tone, making writing polish a load-bearing responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: The request is ambiguous about depth, so explicitly stating assumptions and uncertainties is necessary.
rationale.RX.2: Phrases like “strongest progress guarantee in the literature” risk overclaiming and must be handled cautiously in any cleanup.
rationale.RX.3: Adhering to the chosen scope (polish-only versus technical revisions) avoids unrequested changes.
rationale.RX.4: Misstated concurrency guarantees can mislead practitioners, so edits should reduce potential downstream harm.
rationale.RX.5: All changes should be traceable to the provided text and clearly attributable to maintain provenance.
